channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Smoothie Socials (RSVP)
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 1, 2026 10:00-11:00 AM. The Office of Alumni Engagement invites all students to join us on the first Wednesday of the month for RPI Smoothie Socials. Enjoy delicious smoothies and snacks, connect with fellow students and select staff, and get to know the Alumni Engagement team. Each month features a fun theme, and a unique giveaway for the first 50 students through the door! April’s theme: Welcome Whirl. April’s Giveaway: RPI-branded blender bottle. Register by March 30.
item.author: Students

item.title: CRCC Job and Internship Fair
item.description: It’s not too late to secure an internship or expand your full-time search! Attend the Capital Region Career Consortium Job and Internship fair on April 8. Register via Handshake: https://app.joinhandshake.com/career_fairs/905492b0-2207-4be4-abe1-8c8a4d842386/student_preview
item.author: Students

item.title: RPI Student Film Festival: Submit Your Film!
item.description: The RPI Student Film Festival is back for its fifth annual celebration of student creativity, presented by UPAC Cinema and the Arts Department. This festival provides university-level filmmakers a platform to exhibit their work, bridging cinematic production with critical social, scientific, and intellectual discourse. We are now seeking short film submissions from student creators. Deadline for Entry: March 31, 2026
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Hosting Review Sessions Week of 3/29-4/4
item.description: Week of 3/29- 4/4: Need some last minute review for your upcoming exam? Stop by for a Puck Cookie and a Review Session with our Success Center Tutors! Fluid Mechanics: Sunday, 3/29 (5-7pm) at the Success Center Multivar. Cal. : Monday, 3/30 (7-9pm) at the Success Center Physics II: Monday, 3/30 (7-9pm) in Nason Classroom
item.author: Students

item.title: RPI at Work Mechanical Engineering Review
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 1, 2026 12:00 PM - 12:30 PM - Join RPI at Work faculty on a 30-minute information session covering RPI’s online, project-based Master of Engineering program in Mechanical Engineering. Experienced engineers are asked to take on larger and more complex tasks as they progress in their careers, and this program provides the necessary breadth and depth, no matter where one’s career takes them. Please register to join the Zoom meeting
item.author: Students

item.title: Kosher for Passover Meal Plan
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 2, 2026 - Thu , April 9, 2026 (all day) - There will be a Kosher for Passover meal plan with hot and fresh food at RPI’s Chabad on Campus. This is free for those on the RPI meal plan. However, you will not be able to use them for other dining services during Passover. Alternatively, the early bird special is $200. Scholarships are also available. Please sign up here: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScfh1xJm7W8GGPH_1ugoRrSueNCbW8-w4GkgtsH6wMP4G0AOA/viewform
item.author: Students

item.title: MARCH - APRIL Mercer X CLUBHOUSE WORKSHOP Events
item.description: Bring the heat with March - April Mercer X Workshops: Learn skills to Maximize Your Summer (Andrew Morrison ’87). Design an Integrated Circuit (IEEE) or a Pixie Radio with an Antenna (W2SZ). Learn from experts about cutting-edge AI technology (RPAi). Create your own custom keypad to learn PCB design and physically make it using the Mercer X Printed Circuit Board Prototyping Suite (IEEE). RSVP for these free events held by Mercer X Affiliated Clubs!
item.author: Students

item.title: Lally Excellence in Teaching Award Nominations Now Open
item.description: Recognize a Lally faculty member who made a lasting impact on your academic experience. The Lally Excellence in Teaching Award honors instructors who inspire learning, foster engaging classrooms, and go above and beyond for students. Submit a nomination by April 1 using this link: https://forms.gle/hzGhR2Wjih7NoppB7.
item.author: Students

item.title: Register for Commencement!
item.description: In the Tassel platform, register for commencement. Confirm attendance, approve your name recording, and claim tickets. Registration closes on Sunday, April 19th, 2026. If you have any questions, reach out to commencement@rpi.edu
item.author: Students

item.title: Writing for Fellowships & Grants: Workshop for PhD Students
item.description: Learn approaches to presenting content & editing principles to develop concise effective applications. Previous participants have secured major awards, including international fellowships! April 8th, 2:00-4:00 PM, In person. Register to attend! https://surveys.rpi.edu/jfe/form/SV_6L3FFwv0qCROrvE Questions? madige@rpi.edu
item.author: Community

item.title: MANE Spring Seminar Series with Dr. Jiayong Zhang
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 1, 2026 10:30 AM - 11:30 AM - “Advancing Medical Imaging: Physics and Innovations in Computed Tomography”, Dr. Jiayong Zhang, Research Scientist, Innovation Center, General Electric Healthcare
item.author: Community

item.title: Side by Side Concert 2026
item.description: Tuesday / March 31, 2026 at 7:30 pm The combined forces of the RPI Orchestra and Empire State Youth Orchestra’s Symphony Orchestra unite to perform Gustav Mahler’s epic Symphony no. 1. The program will be led by RPI music faculty Dr. Robert Whalen, and will also include a performance of Edvard Grieg’s famous Concerto for Piano and Orchestra by 2025 Concerto Competition winner Armstrong Wang ’27. Presented by School of HASS Performance Rensselaer Orchestra and ESYO
item.author: Community

item.title: CEE Seminar Series - Dr. Jerome Lutin Retired Senior Director of Statewide & Regional Planning
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 1, 2026 1:00 - 2:00 PM - “Automation and Public Transportation, Past, Present and Future” Public transportation enabled the dynamic growth of cities, but it is expensive to build and operate, and urban development patterns have changed. How have technological changes impacted public transportation, and how will vehicle automation affect transit in the future? This presentation examines how technologies have reshaped transit performance, costs, and the passenger experience.
item.author: Community

item.title: Math Colloquium: Dr. Kurt Polzin from Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 1, 2026 11:00 AM - 12:00 PM - This Mathematical Sciences Colloquium will feature a presentation titled “What is Friction for the Ocean Interior?” by Dr. Kurt Polzin. He is a Physical Oceanographer at Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution (WHOI) a independent non-profit dedicated to ocean science, technology, education, and communication.
item.author: Community

item.title: ECSE Mercer Lecture: “Spatial AI for Robots and Humans”
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 1, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Join us for an ECSE Distinguished Mercer Lecture: “Spatial AI for Robots and Humans” with Prof. John J. Leonard of MIT. Abstract and speaker bio can be found at the event URL.
item.author: Community

item.title: After Sustainability: Climate Change & Authoritarianism - Talk scheduled for April 23
item.description: How is the climate crisis reshaping politics? April Anson (University of Connecticut) and William Callison (Hunter College) join RPI for a public conversation on climate politics and the global rise of authoritarianism. Bringing together political theory and environmental humanities, this event explores the growing connections between climate crisis and far-right politics—and what that may mean for the future. Event starts at 5pm on April 23 in CBIS Auditorium.
item.author: Community

item.title: Department of Economics Spring Seminar Series: Edson Severnini, Boston College
item.description: Please join us as the Department of Economics welcomes Dr. Edson Severnini, who will present: “The Social Lifecycle Impacts of Power Plant Siting in the Historical United States” next Wednesday, April 1st at 3:30 PM in SAGE 5510.
item.author: Community

item.title: Physics, Applied Physics and Astronomy and Biomedical Engeering Host the Robert Resnick Lecture
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 1, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Dr. Xi-Cheng Zhang, Parker Givens Professor of Optics, The Institute of Optics, University of Rochester Title: “THz Photonics: New Spectral Frontier”
item.author: Community

item.title: MSE Seminar: Dr. Cui-Zu Chang
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 1, 2026 11:00 AM - 12:00 PM - “The Art and Science of Molecular Beam Epitaxy —From Quantum Anomalous Hall Effect to Interfacial Superconductivity” Dr. Cui-Zu Chang Department of Physics, Pennsylvania State University
item.author: Community

item.title: Join us for RPI’s Watch Party for NASA’s Artemis II Launch
item.description: Wednesday, April 1, 2026 5:45 PM - 11:00 PM - We warmly invite you to join RPI’s Watch Party for NASA’s Artemis II Launch. The event is scheduled for April 1, 2026, starting at 5:45 PM ET in LOW 3051 (please note that the launch time is subject to change by NASA). We will begin with a few exciting short talks/presentations along with snacks and beverages, and there will also be plenty of fun giveaways/goodies.
item.author: Community

item.title: Jonsson Engineering Center (JEC) to Greene Walkway Closed
item.description: Beginning April 1, 2026, the walkway between the Jonsson Engineering Center (JEC) and the Greene Building will be fully closed. The pathway below the elevated walkway will be closed during the day and open evenings and weekends. Alternate pedestrian routes are required. Please plan your walk accordingly. The entire project is expected to be completed by May 15, 2026.
item.author: Community

item.title: Cognitive Science Colloquium with Dr. Ben Weissman scheduled for April 1st
item.description: Please join the Cognitive Science Department on Wednesday, April 1st, at 12:00 pm in Carnegie 113 for a presentation titled “How we build linguistic representations in social and digital contexts” by Dr. Ben Weissman, Visiting Assistant Professor in the Psychology Department at College of the Holy Cross.
item.author: Community

item.title: Rensselaer Faculty and Staff Quick Learn Webinar: Tips for Improving Online Breakout Room Sessions
item.description:
Date: Tuesday, April 7, 2026 1:30 PM - 2:00 PM - In this webinar we will review tips and tricks to creating more productive breakout sessions. In this session, we will not be learning “how to” use the breakout room feature in Webex but we will be strategizing on how to improve the engagement in the sessions by offering roles, mixing things up, and creating connections. To register for this workshop and see a list of other upcoming workshops, please use the website listed in the See section.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
